Redis Caching Strategy: The application uses Redis 7 as a distributed cache with a cache-aside pattern, where the application checks Redis before querying PostgreSQL and populates the cache on a miss. Cache keys are namespaced by resource type and ID (e.g., user:{id}:profile) with TTLs tuned per resource: user profiles expire after 5 minutes, product catalog data after 60 minutes, and session tokens at their natural expiry time. A Redis Cluster with three primary shards and one replica each provides 99.99% availability and horizontal throughput scaling. Cache stampede protection uses probabilistic early expiration and a distributed lock (via Redlock) on cache population for high-traffic keys.
